rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HANS HERBERT BARTSCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition And For Protective Order Barring Further Litigation Activities By Executor; For Sanctions And Award Of Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner Herterich's Motion to Quash Deposition of Morgi and for an Order Barring Further Litigation Activities by Executor is denied. CCP 2026.010 controls the taking of depositions outside California. CCP 2026.010(c) provides that a party serving a deposition notice on a non-party "shall use any process and procedures required and available under the laws of the state . . . where the deposition is to be taken. New Jersey Rules of Court 4:14-7 provides that the attendance of a witness at the taking of depositions may be compelled by subpoena. Morgi consented to be deposed. Secondary New Jersey authorities reflect that non-parties attendance at a deposition can be procured by the consent of the deponent or by a subpoena. (See e.g. 42 N.J. Prac., Discovery 4.62.) CCP 2025.240(c) relates to oral depositions within California and provides if the attendance of the deponent is to be compelled by service of a deposition subpoena, a copy shall be served with the deposition notice. However, 2026.010, et seq. is controlling. Inadmissibility of Morgi's testimony and/or any documents produced at her deposition, assuming it can be demonstrated at trial, does not preclude the taking of the deposition, and is not the test to be applied to the scope of discovery. The deposition of Morgi is not an undue burden and expense. The deposition of Morgi will assist the court in the determination of the pending heirship petition and the Court therefore denies the request for an Order Barring Further Litigation Activities by Executor without prejudice; and, without prejudice to objection at the time of any future petition for attorney fees incurred by executor which must demonstrate the activities of the Executor assist the court as set forth in Estate of Bartsch (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 885. The Petitioner's and Respondent's requests for sanctions and attorney fees are denied. Any party who wishes to argue the tentative must contact the court (415-551-3702) and opp
